title: One Thousand and Twenty-Four
summary: A knowledge base search returns confident but completely wrong answers. The documents are correct, the model is fine.

personality.demeanor: methodical, speaks in specifics, references precision and recall like a reflex
personality.recurring_concern: client trust in legal research accuracy

facts: partner at Whitfield & Associates called in directly
their associate used the assistant to prepare a brief and cited a case from an unrelated jurisdiction
QA team ran the regression suite
retrieval precision dropped from 0.91 to 0.12 overnight
every query returns results but results are essentially random
CTO asking whether to take the assistant offline
two more firms reported incorrect citations this morning
knowledge base sync succeeded and S3 source documents are correct
issue is in how vectors are stored and matched, not in the source data

glossary.Knowledge Base: A managed service in Amazon Bedrock that connects your documents to a large language model. It splits documents into chunks, converts them to vectors, stores them in a vector database, and retrieves relevant chunks when a user asks a question.
glossary.Vector Embedding: A list of numbers that represents the meaning of a piece of text. Similar texts produce similar lists of numbers. An embedding model converts human-readable text into these numerical representations so machines can compare meaning mathematically.
glossary.Dimension: The length of a vector embedding -- how many numbers are in the list. Amazon Titan Embeddings V2 can produce vectors with 256, 512, or 1,024 dimensions. Higher dimensions capture more nuance but use more storage.
glossary.knn_vector: A field type in OpenSearch that stores vector embeddings and supports nearest-neighbor search. The dimension parameter is set when the index is created and cannot be changed afterward.
glossary.OpenSearch Serverless Collection: A managed group of OpenSearch indexes that automatically scales without you provisioning or managing servers. Collections can be configured for vector search, time series, or general search workloads.
glossary.Data Source Sync: The process where Bedrock reads documents from a source like S3, splits them into chunks, generates vector embeddings for each chunk, and writes those vectors into the vector store. A sync can complete with zero errors even if the vectors are semantically wrong.
glossary.Retrieval Precision: The fraction of retrieved results that are actually relevant to the query. A precision of 0.91 means 91 out of 100 returned documents were relevant. A precision of 0.12 means the system is returning mostly irrelevant results.
glossary.Silent Truncation: When a vector with more dimensions than the index allows is written, the extra dimensions are dropped without any error, warning, or log entry. The remaining partial vector has no coherent semantic meaning.
glossary.HNSW: Hierarchical Navigable Small World -- an algorithm used by OpenSearch to build a graph structure for fast approximate nearest-neighbor search. It is configured per-index with parameters like ef_construction and m.

narrative_arc.call: alert arrives, stakes established from story.md Opening
narrative_arc.threshold: retrieval logs show every query returning results with low uniform scores (0.30-0.35), no errors, sync completed cleanly, S3 documents intact -- problem somewhere between documents and search results
narrative_arc.trials: sync job shows 280,412 documents indexed with zero failures, CloudWatch metrics show normal latency and zero errors, obvious suspects (bad source data, failed ingestion, broken index) all check out -- system looks healthy by every standard metric
narrative_arc.revelation: embedding model configuration says 1,024 dimensions, OpenSearch index mapping says 512, model upgraded Thursday, index never recreated, every vector silently truncated in half since sync ran
narrative_arc.return: delete index and recreate with 1,024 dimensions, full re-sync, add CI check comparing model dimensions to index dimensions and CloudWatch alarm on retrieval precision

components.name: terravox-legal-kb (Bedrock Knowledge Base)
components.role: Orchestrates the RAG pipeline: receives queries, generates query embeddings, retrieves relevant chunks from the vector store, and passes them to the generation model
connections: Amazon Titan Embeddings V2 for embedding generation
terravox-legal-vectors OpenSearch Serverless collection for vector storage and retrieval
terravox-legal-docs-prod S3 bucket as document source
components.failure_impact: If the knowledge base is misconfigured or inactive, the RetrieveAndGenerate API returns errors and the assistant cannot answer questions

components.name: Amazon Titan Embeddings V2 (embedding model)
components.role: Converts text chunks and queries into numerical vector embeddings. Currently configured to produce 1,024-dimension vectors
connections: terravox-legal-kb triggers embedding generation during sync and at query time
components.failure_impact: If the model dimension does not match the index dimension, vectors are silently truncated during ingestion and search returns random results

components.name: terravox-legal-vectors (OpenSearch Serverless collection)
components.role: Stores vector embeddings in the terravox-legal-index and performs approximate nearest-neighbor search at query time
connections: terravox-legal-kb writes vectors during sync and reads vectors during retrieval
components.failure_impact: If the collection is inactive or the index mapping is wrong, ingestion or retrieval fails or produces garbage results

components.name: terravox-legal-index (OpenSearch index, knn_vector dimension: 512)
components.role: The actual index within the collection that defines the vector field mapping, dimension, HNSW parameters, and stores the indexed vectors
components.failure_impact: If the dimension parameter does not match the embedding model output, vectors are truncated on write. The index cannot be altered in place -- it must be deleted and recreated.

components.name: terravox-legal-docs-prod (S3 bucket)
components.role: Stores 280,412 legal documents organized by jurisdiction and case type. Serves as the source for knowledge base ingestion
connections: terravox-legal-kb reads documents during data source sync
components.failure_impact: If documents are missing, corrupted, or the bucket policy blocks Bedrock access, the sync fails or indexes incomplete data

components.name: CloudWatch metrics and logs
components.role: Monitors API latency, ingestion job status, search latency, and custom retrieval quality metrics
connections: Bedrock publishes ingestion and retrieval metrics
Custom metrics for precision and recall
components.failure_impact: If retrieval precision is not alarmed, quality regressions go undetected while all health metrics remain green

system_narration.data_flow: Law firm users query the Terravox assistant via API Gateway and Lambda. Lambda calls the Bedrock RetrieveAndGenerate API, which embeds the query using Titan V2 at 1,024 dimensions, searches the OpenSearch Serverless index for nearest neighbors, retrieves matching text chunks, and passes them to the generation model. During ingestion, Bedrock reads documents from S3, chunks them, embeds each chunk, and writes the vectors to the OpenSearch index.
system_narration.what_broke: On Thursday at 14:30, the embedding model configuration was changed from 512 to 1,024 dimensions. The automated data source sync ran at 18:00 and completed without error. But the OpenSearch index terravox-legal-index still had dimension: 512 in its knn_vector mapping. Every 1,024-dimension vector was silently truncated to 512 values on write. The truncated vectors lost their semantic meaning. Nearest-neighbor searches returned results with near-random similarity scores (0.29-0.35). No error was logged. No alarm fired. The failure was invisible to every metric except retrieval precision, which was not wired to an alarm.

hints.hint: retrieval returns results for every query but returns the wrong ones -- what could cause a vector search to return near-random matches?
something changed in the embedding pipeline recently -- compare the current embedding model configuration to the OpenSearch index mapping
embedding model now produces 1,024-dimension vectors -- how many dimensions does the OpenSearch index expect?
index mapping specifies 512 dimensions, model produces 1,024, vectors are being truncated during ingestion destroying their semantic meaning

resolution.root_cause: The Bedrock Knowledge Base embedding model was reconfigured from Amazon Titan Embeddings V2 at 512 dimensions to 1,024 dimensions, but the OpenSearch Serverless index knn_vector mapping was never updated from its original 512-dimension definition, causing vectors to be silently truncated during ingestion and destroying semantic similarity.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies the mismatch between the embedding model's 1,024-dimension output and the OpenSearch index's 512-dimension mapping
Player understands that the dimension mismatch causes vectors to lose semantic meaning, making retrieval effectively random
Player proposes recreating the OpenSearch index with the correct 1,024-dimension mapping and re-syncing the knowledge base
Player notes that the data source sync should validate dimension compatibility or that a CI check should compare model config to index schema

learning_objectives: How vector dimensions in embedding models must match the vector store index configuration
OpenSearch Serverless index mappings and knn_vector field configuration for Bedrock Knowledge Bases
Silent failure modes in RAG pipelines where ingestion succeeds but retrieval quality degrades
Importance of retrieval quality metrics (precision, recall) as operational monitoring alongside availability metrics
sop_steps: 1. Acknowledge the incident. Law firms are reporting that the Terravox legal research assistant is returning irrelevant case citations.

2. Check whether the knowledge base is running. Use get-knowledge-base to confirm the Bedrock Knowledge Base (terravox-legal-kb, ID TVXKB7R2NQ) is in ACTIVE status. A knowledge base is a managed service that connects your documents to an AI model for question-answering.

3. Check whether the latest data import succeeded. Use list-ingestion-jobs to review the most recent sync job (TVXIJ20260320). An ingestion job reads documents from S3, splits them into chunks, converts each chunk into a vector (a list of numbers representing its meaning), and stores those vectors in a search index. Confirm it completed with zero failures.

4. Check the quality of search results. Look at the RetrievalPrecision and RetrievalRecall metrics in CloudWatch. Precision measures how many of the returned results are actually relevant. Recall measures how many of the relevant results were found. Identify the drop from 0.91/0.87 to 0.12/0.09 after the sync -- search results are now essentially random.

5. Check how many numbers are in each vector the model produces. Inspect the Bedrock Knowledge Base embedding model configuration. The dimensions field is set to 1024, meaning each text chunk is converted into a list of 1,024 numbers.

6. Check how many numbers the search index expects. Inspect the OpenSearch Serverless index mapping for terravox-legal-index. The knn_vector dimension field is set to 512 -- the index only accepts lists of 512 numbers.

7. Identify the mismatch. The model produces 1,024-number vectors, but the index only accepts 512. When a longer vector is written to an index with a smaller dimension, the extra numbers are silently chopped off. This destroys the meaning encoded in the vector, making search results random.

8. Delete the existing index. The OpenSearch Serverless index (terravox-legal-index) cannot have its dimension changed in place -- the dimension is locked at creation time. You must delete and recreate it.

9. Recreate the index with the correct size. Set dimension to 1024 in the knn_vector field mapping. Keep the same search algorithm settings (HNSW with ef_construction: 512 and m: 16). HNSW is the algorithm OpenSearch uses to find similar vectors quickly.
10. Re-import all documents. Trigger a full data source re-sync on the knowledge base to re-embed and re-index all 280,412 documents with the correct 1,024-dimension vectors.
11. Verify the fix. Monitor the ingestion job until it completes, then run the QA regression suite to confirm retrieval precision returns to baseline (0.91 or higher).

12. Prevent this from happening again. Add a CI validation step that automatically compares the embedding model's dimension configuration against the OpenSearch index mapping dimension before any deployment reaches production.
13. Set up a quality alarm. Wire the RetrievalPrecision custom CloudWatch metric to an alarm that triggers if precision drops below 0.80. This catches quality problems before users do.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Someone swaps the embedding model to a completely different one with a different vector size
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Instead of changing a setting on the same model, someone replaces the model entirely -- for example, switching from Amazon Titan V2 to a Cohere model. The new model produces vectors of a different size. The mismatch effect is the same (silent truncation, random results), but the cause is a model swap rather than a configuration change, which may be harder to notice in a code review.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Add a pre-deployment check that looks up the embedding model, determines how many dimensions it produces, and compares that number against the vector store index mapping before any knowledge base update goes live.

related_failure_modes.scenario: The encryption key for the OpenSearch collection is rotated or deleted, blocking all access
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The collection is encrypted using a KMS key (a managed encryption key from AWS). If that key becomes unavailable -- deleted, disabled, or its permissions changed -- both ingestion and search fail with access denied errors. Unlike the dimension mismatch, this failure is loud and obvious: nothing works, and the error messages point directly to the key.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Use KMS key policies that prevent accidental deletion. Set up CloudWatch alarms on KMS key state changes so you know immediately if a key is disabled or scheduled for deletion.

related_failure_modes.scenario: The document splitting settings are changed but old documents keep their original chunks
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The chunking strategy (how documents are split into smaller pieces for indexing) is modified -- for example, changing the chunk size or the overlap between chunks. But only newly added documents get the new chunking. Existing documents keep their old chunk boundaries. Search quality degrades gradually rather than catastrophically, making the problem harder to detect.
related_failure_modes.prevention: After any change to chunking settings, run a full re-sync (not incremental) so all documents are re-split with the new strategy. Monitor retrieval quality metrics continuously.

related_failure_modes.scenario: The OpenSearch collection runs out of capacity and silently drops some vectors
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: OpenSearch Serverless collections have capacity limits measured in OCUs (OpenSearch Compute Units). If the collection runs out of indexing capacity, some vectors are indexed and others are quietly skipped. Unlike the dimension mismatch (where all vectors are bad), here some search results are correct and others are simply missing. The ingestion job may show fewer documents indexed than scanned.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Monitor OpenSearch Serverless OCU consumption and set alarms when utilization exceeds 80%. After every sync job, compare the documents-scanned count to the documents-indexed count to catch dropped documents.

sop_practices: Before deploying any change to the embedding model or the vector store index, verify that the number of dimensions matches on both sides. The model produces vectors of a certain size; the index must be configured to accept exactly that size. Automate this comparison in your CI/CD pipeline so a mismatch is caught before it reaches production.

Treat search quality metrics as real operational metrics, not just experiment numbers. Precision (how many results are relevant), recall (how many relevant results were found), and NDCG (a ranking quality score) should have CloudWatch alarms with the same urgency as latency and error rate alarms.

After any change to the embedding model, the document splitting strategy, or the index configuration, run a full data source re-sync followed by a regression test suite. Do not assume an incremental sync (which only processes new or changed documents) will preserve quality -- old documents may now have incompatible vectors.

Keep a single source of truth that documents the relationship between your embedding model configuration (which model, how many dimensions) and your vector store index schema (which dimension the index expects). When one changes, the other must be reviewed.
